assertion_results.text: Sibling check correctly classifies TC-8001 as a different-stream companion (stream [rhtpa-2.2] vs current issue's [rhtpa-2.1]), not a same-stream duplicate (Step 4)
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md lines 17-23: 'Current issue TC-8006 has stream suffix [rhtpa-2.1] (stream 2.1.x). Sibling TC-8001 has stream suffix [rhtpa-2.2] (stream 2.2.x). Classification: Different-stream companion (NOT a duplicate).' Step 4.1 confirms: 'No same-stream siblings found. TC-8001 is in a different stream (2.2.x vs 2.1.x). No duplicate closure warranted.'

assertion_results.text: Step 4.2 checks the current issue's existing issuelinks before attempting to create a 'Related' link to TC-8001 (§1.58)
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md lines 33-39: 'Before creating a "Related" link to sibling TC-8001, checking the current issue's existing issuelinks array (fetched in Step 1 via jira.get_issue).' The existing links table is displayed showing Link ID 1990401, Type Related, outward, TC-8001. data-extraction.md lines 37-43 also records the existing issuelinks from Step 1 for use in Step 4.2.

assertion_results.text: Step 4.2 detects the pre-existing 'Related' link to TC-8001 and skips link creation — does NOT call jira.create_link (§1.58)
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md lines 45-49: 'Result: Pre-existing "Related" link to TC-8001 found. > Related link to TC-8001 already exists -- skipping. Link creation is skipped to avoid creating a duplicate link.' triage-outcome.md lines 42-44: 'Link creation was skipped. No jira.create_link call was made. This prevents creating a duplicate "Related" link between TC-8006 and TC-8001.'

assertion_results.text: The output includes a log message indicating the link was skipped because it already exists (e.g., 'Related link to TC-8001 already exists — skipping')
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md line 47: '> Related link to TC-8001 already exists -- skipping'. triage-outcome.md line 42: '> Related link to TC-8001 already exists -- skipping'. The message matches the expected pattern using em-dashes instead of en-dashes but conveys the same meaning.

assertion_results.text: The sibling landscape table is still presented to the engineer despite the link already existing — the idempotent check only affects link creation, not the sibling summary
assertion_results.evidence: sibling-check.md lines 59-67: The 'Sibling Landscape' table is present showing both TC-8001 (2.2.x, In Progress) and TC-8006 (2.1.x, New), with explicit note: 'The sibling landscape is presented despite the link already existing, as it provides useful context for the engineer.' triage-outcome.md lines 49-57 reiterates: 'The landscape table is always shown regardless of link status because it provides valuable context about the overall CVE tracking across streams. Skipping the link creation does not skip the informational output.'
